name: Browse...
short_description: Opens a directory requester to select the folder to tidy.
usage_tip: The selected path is remembered for your session, so you can easily re-tidy after adjusting settings.
notes: Uses ASL file requester with ASLFR_DrawersOnly flag. Updates folder_path_buffer and redraws folder path display box.

name: Start
short_description: Starts the tidying process using the current settings and selected folder.
usage_tip: Always review your settings and enable backups before processing important folders.
notes: Triggers ProcessDirectoryWithPreferencesAndProgress(). Opens progress window during execution. Respects backup, recursive, sort, and window position settings.

name: Cancel
short_description: Closes the iTidy main window without making any changes.
notes: Sets continue_running = FALSE to exit event loop.

name: Order
short_description: Controls how icons are grouped: Folders First, Files First, or Mixed.
usage_tip: Folders First is best for project trees; Mixed works well for drawers with few files.
notes: Sets prefs->sortPriority. Options: FOLDERS_FIRST, FILES_FIRST, MIXED.

name: Sort by
short_description: Chooses the sorting criteria: Name, Type, Date, or Size.
usage_tip: Type sorting groups similar file types together; Date is useful for chronological archives.
notes: Sets prefs->sortBy. Options: NAME, TYPE, DATE, SIZE.

name: Position
short_description: Controls where drawer windows appear after resizing: Center Screen, Keep Position, Near Parent, or No Change.
usage_tip: Use No Change if you only want icons tidied without moving or resizing windows.
notes: Sets prefs->windowPositionMode. See GID_WINDOW_POSITION_HELP for detailed explanations.

short_description: Opens a help dialog explaining the window positioning options.
notes: Displays EasyRequest with descriptions of Center Screen, Keep Position, Near Parent, and No Change modes.

name: Cleanup subfolders
short_description: When checked, iTidy processes all subdirectories within the selected folder recursively.
usage_tip: Great for tidying entire partitions, but can take time on slower systems with many folders.
notes: Sets prefs->recursive_subdirs. Uses ProcessDirectoryRecursive() when enabled.

name: Backup icons
short_description: Creates LhA backups of all .info files before making changes, allowing you to restore later.
usage_tip: Always enable this for large or recursive operations to ensure you can safely undo changes.
notes: Sets prefs->enable_backup and prefs->backupPrefs.enableUndoBackup. Requires LhA in C: directory. Creates backups in PROGDIR:Backups/.

name: Advanced...
short_description: Opens the Advanced Settings window for fine-grained control over layout, spacing, and window sizing.
usage_tip: Use this after testing basic settings to customize aspect ratios, icon spacing, and overflow behavior.
notes: Opens modal iTidyAdvancedWindow. If changes accepted, updates global preferences.

name: Fix Default Tools...
short_description: Opens the Default Tool Analysis window to scan and repair broken default tool paths in icons.
usage_tip: Run this if you see 'Unable to open your tool' errors when double-clicking icons.
notes: Syncs current folder path to global prefs before opening tool cache window. Opens modal iTidyToolCacheWindow.

name: Restore Backups...
short_description: Opens the Restore Backups window to revert icon positions and window layouts from previous backups.
usage_tip: Use this to undo changes if you're not happy with how a folder was tidied.
notes: Only restores icon layout backups created by 'Backup icons' option. Does not restore default tool changes. Opens modal iTidyRestoreWindow.

name: New
short_description: Resets all settings to default values.
usage_tip: Use this to start fresh if you've customized many settings and want to return to defaults.
notes: Calls handle_main_new_menu(). Resets global preferences to defaults.

name: Open...
short_description: Loads previously saved iTidy settings from a file.
usage_tip: Save different configurations for different use cases, then quickly switch between them.
notes: Opens ASL file requester. Loads binary LayoutPreferences file. Updates GUI to reflect loaded settings.

name: Save
short_description: Saves current settings to the last saved file, or prompts for a filename if not yet saved.
usage_tip: Quick-save your favorite configuration after finding settings you like.
notes: Uses last_save_path if available, otherwise behaves like Save As. Saves binary LayoutPreferences structure.

name: Save as...
short_description: Saves current settings to a new file with a custom name.
usage_tip: Create multiple preset files for different workflows, like 'WHDLoad_Layout.prefs' or 'Tight_Grid.prefs'.
notes: Opens ASL save requester. Saves binary file with header 'ITIDYPREFS' and version number.

name: Close
short_description: Closes the iTidy main window and exits the program.
notes: Returns FALSE from menu handler to signal application exit.

name: Aspect Ratio
short_description: Sets the target width-to-height ratio for drawer windows: Tall, Square, Compact, Classic, Wide, or Ultrawide.
usage_tip: Wide (2.0) works well on modern displays; Square (1.0) is good for small screens or tall folder lists.
notes: Sets prefs->aspectRatio. Presets: 0.75, 1.0, 1.3, 1.6, 2.0, 2.4. Stored as fixed-point (multiplied by 1000).

name: Overflow Mode
short_description: Controls how iTidy handles folders with too many icons: Expand Horizontally, Expand Vertically, or Expand Both.
usage_tip: Expand Horizontally (default) is best for wide screens; Expand Vertically suits tall displays.
notes: Sets prefs->overflowMode. Options: OVERFLOW_HORIZONTAL, OVERFLOW_VERTICAL, OVERFLOW_BOTH.

name: Auto Max Icons
short_description: When checked, iTidy automatically calculates the maximum number of columns based on screen width.
usage_tip: Leave enabled for automatic responsive layouts; disable for manual control via Max Icons/Row.
notes: Disables/enables GID_ADV_MAX_ICONS_ROW gadget. When enabled, uses screen width and Max Window Width % to calculate columns.

name: Max Window Width
short_description: Limits drawer window width as a percentage of screen width: Auto, 30%, 50%, 70%, 90%, or 100%.
usage_tip: Use 70% or 90% to prevent windows from filling the entire screen width.
notes: Only applies when Auto Max Icons is enabled. Sets prefs->maxWindowWidthPct.

name: Align Icons Vertically
short_description: Controls vertical alignment when icons in a row have different heights: Top, Middle, or Bottom.
usage_tip: Middle (default) looks most balanced for mixed icon sizes.
notes: Sets prefs->textAlignment. Options: TOP, MIDDLE, BOTTOM.

name: Reverse Sort (Z->A)
short_description: When checked, reverses the sort order (Z to A, newest first, largest first).
usage_tip: Useful for putting most recent files or largest items at the top of the list.
notes: Sets prefs->reverseSort.

name: Optimize Column Widths
short_description: When checked, each column width is based on its widest icon rather than using a uniform width.
usage_tip: Enabled by default; creates more compact layouts by avoiding wasted space in narrow columns.
notes: Sets prefs->useColumnWidthOptimization.

name: Column Layout (centered)
short_description: When checked, icons are centered within their column instead of left-aligned.
usage_tip: Centered icons look more balanced, especially with Optimize Column Widths enabled.
notes: Sets prefs->centerIconsInColumn.

name: Skip Hidden Folders
short_description: When checked, skips folders without .info files during recursive processing.
usage_tip: Enabled by default; prevents iTidy from processing system or hidden directories.
notes: Sets prefs->skipHiddenFolders. Folders without .info files are typically system folders not meant for Workbench display.

name: Strip NewIcon Borders
short_description: Removes decorative borders from NewIcon format icons during processing.
usage_tip: Only use if you understand NewIcon formats; this is a one-way change that cannot be undone without backups.
notes: Requires icon.library v44+. Sets flag in preferences. This modifies icon image data permanently.

name: Spacing X
short_description: Sets the horizontal spacing between icons in pixels (4-20).
usage_tip: Lower values create tighter layouts; default is 8 pixels.
notes: Sets prefs->iconSpacingX. Range: 4-20 pixels.

name: Spacing Y
short_description: Sets the vertical spacing between icons in pixels (4-20).
usage_tip: Lower values create tighter layouts; default is 8 pixels.
notes: Sets prefs->iconSpacingY. Range: 4-20 pixels.

name: Min Icons/Row
short_description: Sets the minimum number of columns (1-10). Prevents single-column vertical stacks.
usage_tip: Default is 2; increase if you want wider windows even for folders with few icons.
notes: Sets prefs->minIconsPerRow. Range: 1-10.

name: Max Icons/Row
short_description: Sets the maximum number of columns (1-20). Only used when Auto Max Icons is disabled.
usage_tip: Disable Auto Max and set this manually for consistent column counts across all folders.
notes: Sets prefs->maxIconsPerRow. Only active when GID_ADV_MAX_AUTO_CHECKBOX is unchecked.

name: Beta Options...
short_description: Opens the Beta Options window for experimental features and logging settings.
usage_tip: Only use these options if you need debugging output or want to test experimental Workbench window update features.
notes: Opens modal iTidyBetaOptionsWindow.

name: OK
short_description: Saves all advanced settings changes and closes the window.
notes: Sets changes_accepted = TRUE and copies gadget values to preferences structure.

name: Cancel
short_description: Discards all changes and closes the window without saving.
notes: Sets changes_accepted = FALSE. No changes are applied to global preferences.

name: Open folders after processing
short_description: Experimental: Attempts to open processed folders automatically in Workbench.
usage_tip: This is experimental and may not work reliably on all systems.
notes: Sets prefs->beta_openFoldersAfterProcessing. May not be fully functional.

name: Update Workbench windows
short_description: Experimental: Attempts to refresh open Workbench windows after processing.
usage_tip: May not work reliably; a Workbench restart often works better for seeing window changes.
notes: Sets prefs->beta_FindWindowOnWorkbenchAndUpdate. Uses window_enumerator.c functions.

name: Log Level
short_description: Controls verbosity of log files: DEBUG (Verbose), INFO (Normal), WARNING (Warnings only), or ERROR (Errors only).
usage_tip: Use DEBUG for troubleshooting; INFO for normal operation; ERROR to reduce log file size.
notes: Sets prefs->logLevel. Options: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR.

name: Memory Logging
short_description: Enables detailed memory allocation tracking for debugging memory leaks.
usage_tip: Only enable for debugging; creates large log files and slows performance.
notes: Sets prefs->memoryLoggingEnabled. Uses whd_malloc/whd_free tracking system.

name: Performance Logging
short_description: Enables timing measurements for performance analysis.
usage_tip: Use to identify slow operations on large folder sets or real hardware.
notes: Sets prefs->enable_performance_logging.

name: OK
short_description: Saves beta options changes and closes the window.
notes: Sets changes_accepted = TRUE.

name: Cancel
short_description: Discards changes and closes the window.
notes: Sets changes_accepted = FALSE.

name: Tool List
short_description: Shows all discovered default tools, number of icons using each, and whether the tool exists on the system.
usage_tip: Select a tool to see which icons use it in the details list below.
notes: Populated by BuildDefaultToolList(). Columns: Tool Name, Files (hit count), Status (EXISTS/MISSING). Supports double-click to show details.

name: Details List
short_description: Shows all icon files that use the selected default tool from the tool list above.
usage_tip: Select a specific icon file to update just that one, or use Replace Tool (Batch) to update all at once.
notes: Populated when a tool is selected in GID_TOOL_LIST. Shows full .info file paths.

name: Filter
short_description: Filters the tool list to show All tools, Existing tools only, or Missing tools only.
usage_tip: Use 'Missing only' to quickly find broken default tools that need fixing.
notes: Options: All, Existing, Missing. Reapplies filter to tool cache and refreshes display.

name: Browse...
short_description: Opens a directory requester to select the folder to scan for default tools.
usage_tip: Scanning is always recursive, so selecting a top-level partition will scan all subdirectories.
notes: Updates folder path display. Scan is triggered separately via GID_TOOL_SCAN_BOTTOM or menu.

name: Scan
short_description: Scans the selected folder recursively for all WBPROJECT icons and validates their default tools.
usage_tip: Large scans can take several minutes on real hardware; the window shows a busy pointer during scanning.
notes: Calls BuildDefaultToolList() which scans recursively. Populates g_ToolCache. Updates both listviews.

name: Replace Tool (Batch)
short_description: Opens a window to replace the default tool for all icons currently using the selected tool.
usage_tip: Use this to fix all broken icons in one operation after selecting a missing tool.
notes: Opens iTidy_DefaultToolUpdateWindow in UPDATE_MODE_BATCH. Automatically backs up original default tools before changes.

name: Replace Tool (Single)
short_description: Opens a window to replace the default tool for only the selected icon file in the details list.
usage_tip: Use this for one-off fixes when only a single icon needs updating.
notes: Opens iTidy_DefaultToolUpdateWindow in UPDATE_MODE_SINGLE. Requires selection in GID_TOOL_DETAILS_LIST.

name: Restore Default Tools Backups...
short_description: Opens a window to restore original default tool paths from automatic backups created before changes.
usage_tip: Use this to undo default tool changes; it does not restore icon positions or window layouts.
notes: Opens iTidy_CreateToolRestoreWindow. Separate from icon layout backups (those use Restore Backups in main window).

name: Close
short_description: Closes the Default Tool Analysis window.
notes: Returns to main window. Tool cache data (g_ToolCache) remains in memory.

name: New
short_description: Clears the current tool cache and starts fresh.
usage_tip: Use this to reset before scanning a different folder or partition.
notes: Frees g_ToolCache and resets g_ToolCacheCount to 0. Clears both listviews.

name: Open...
short_description: Loads a previously saved tool cache scan from a file.
usage_tip: Useful for large drives that take time to scan; save once and reload later.
notes: Opens ASL file requester. Loads binary tool cache file (header: ITIDYTOOLCACHE, version 2).

name: Save
short_description: Saves the current tool cache scan to the last saved file.
usage_tip: Quick-save after completing a long scan to avoid re-scanning later.
notes: Uses last_save_path if available. Saves binary file with tool names, paths, hit counts, and referencing files.

name: Save as...
short_description: Saves the current tool cache scan to a new file with a custom name.
usage_tip: Create named scans for different partitions, like 'DH0_Tools.cache' or 'Work_Tools.cache'.
notes: Opens ASL save requester. Binary format includes folder path, tool data, and file references.

name: Close
short_description: Closes the Default Tool Analysis window.
notes: Returns FALSE from menu handler to close window.

name: Export list of tools
short_description: Exports the tool list to a text file showing tool names, file counts, and status.
usage_tip: Use to create a report of all default tools found on a partition.
notes: Opens ASL save requester. Creates formatted text file with tool names and statistics.

name: Export list of files and tools
short_description: Exports a detailed list showing each icon file and its default tool.
usage_tip: Use for comprehensive reports listing every scanned icon and its tool path.
notes: Opens ASL save requester. Creates formatted text file with icon paths and their default tools.

name: System PATH...
short_description: Displays the parsed Amiga PATH used for validating simple tool names.
usage_tip: Use to verify iTidy is finding tools correctly based on your system PATH configuration.
notes: Parses S:Startup-Sequence and S:User-Startup. Shows PATH entries in a scrollable window.

name: Browse...
short_description: Opens a file requester to select the new default tool program.
usage_tip: Navigate to the correct program on your system, like SYS:Utilities/MultiView.
notes: Uses ASL file requester with pattern #?. Updates new_path_string gadget with selected path.

name: Update
short_description: Applies the new default tool to all selected icons, creating backups before making changes.
usage_tip: Always verify the new tool path before clicking Update to avoid replacing with another incorrect path.
notes: Calls perform_tool_update(). Creates backups via iTidy_ToolBackupManager. Updates status listview with results (SUCCESS/FAILED/READ-ONLY).

name: Close
short_description: Closes the Default Tool Update window.
notes: Returns to Default Tool Analysis window. If updates were made, parent window refreshes tool cache.

name: Run List
short_description: Shows all available backup runs with date, folder count, size, and completion status.
usage_tip: Double-click a run to restore it quickly, or single-click to view details first.
notes: Populated by scan_backup_runs(). Columns: Run, Date/Time, Folders, Size, Status. Supports double-click to trigger restore.

name: Restore window positions
short_description: When checked, restores both icon positions and window geometry (size and position).
usage_tip: Checked by default; a Workbench restart may be needed to see window size and position changes.
notes: Passed to restore operation. When enabled, restores .info window metadata in addition to icon positions.

name: Restore Run
short_description: Extracts all .info files from the selected backup run to their original locations.
usage_tip: This overwrites current icon positions; make a new backup first if you want to preserve current state.
notes: Requires LhA in C:. Opens progress window during extraction. Calls RestoreBackupRun() from backup_restore.c.

name: Delete Run
short_description: Permanently deletes the selected backup run and all its archive files.
usage_tip: Use this to free disk space from old backups you no longer need; deletion cannot be undone.
notes: Shows confirmation requester before deletion. Removes run directory and all .lha archives. Rescans backup directory after deletion.

name: View Folders...
short_description: Opens a window showing all folders included in the selected backup run.
usage_tip: Use this to verify which folders are backed up before restoring, especially for large recursive backups.
notes: Opens modal folder view window. Parses catalog.txt and displays folder paths in a listview.

name: Cancel
short_description: Closes the Restore Backups window without making any changes.
notes: Returns to main window. Sets restore_performed flag if any restores were done.
